name: Zufre, Embalse de Zufre – circular in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park
name_original: Zufre, Embalse de Zufre – circular por el Parque Natural Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche
region_intro: Road cycling around Zufre, a village situated at 450 meters above sea level within the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park, offers diverse terrain. The landscape features gentle mountains, lush olive groves, and expansive pastures, alongside holm oak and cork oak groves. Numerous streams wind through the area, and the large Zufre Reservoir is a prominent natural feature, providing scenic views from various points. Best road cycling routes around Zufre The most popular road cycling route is Zufre, Santa Olalla and Al, a 68.1 miles (109.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 28 minutes to complete. This difficult route features demanding climbs and picturesque vistas across the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Zufre, Embalse de Zufre – circular in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park, a moderate 32.1 miles (51.6 km) path. This route offers views of the Zufre Reservoir and the surrounding natural park. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto Moral, Higuera de la Sierra, Zufre, Cala – circular route, a 44.2 miles (71.1 km) trail leading through rolling hills and natural park scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Zufre is defined by gentle mountains, lush olive groves, and the scenic Zufre Reservoir. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Zufre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Zufre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Zufre?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Zufre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 1 easy route, 12 moderate routes, and 33 difficult routes, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Zufre?
A: Zufre, located in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park, offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling, allowing you to enjoy the lush landscapes without extreme heat. Summer can be warm, but early morning rides are still very enjoyable.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Zufre?
A: Yes, Zufre is known for its demanding climbs and picturesque vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994025282/zufre-santa-olalla-and-al" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zufre, Santa Olalla and Al&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 68.1-mile trail featuring over 1,700 meters of elevation gain, perfect for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Zufre?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991328807/zufre-embalse-de-zufre-circular-in-the-sierra-de-aracena-y-picos-de-aroche-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zufre, Embalse de Zufre – circular in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 32.1-mile path offering scenic views of the reservoir and natural park.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Zufre often feature stunning natural landscapes, including holm oak and cork oak groves, olive groves, and gentle mountains. The prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3967733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zufre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a frequent scenic highlight. You might also encounter other natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotto of the Marvels&lt;/a&gt; in nearby Aracena, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to explore while cycling near Zufre?
A: Yes, Zufre itself is a historic 'white village' with an Islamic heritage. You can explore its narrow streets, the Church of the Immaculate Conception, and the unique elliptical Zufre Bullring. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300354" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotto of the Marvels&lt;/a&gt; in Aracena is also a significant nearby attraction.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on road cycling routes in Zufre?
A: You'll experience a diverse natural backdrop characterized by gentle mountains, lush olive groves, and expansive pastures. The landscape is further enriched by holm oak and cork oak groves, chestnut trees, and scrubland, with numerous streams winding through the terrain. The large Zufre Reservoir also provides magnificent views from various points.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route in Zufre?
A: As a charming village, Zufre offers various public parking options, particularly near the historic center or on the outskirts. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas or consult local signage upon arrival to find the most convenient spots for starting your ride.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Zufre, being a local hub, has cafes and establishments where you can refuel. Many routes pass through or near other small villages in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park, which often have local bars or shops for refreshments. Planning your route with komoot can help identify potential stops.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Zufre?
A: The road cycling routes around Zufre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Zufre's varied terrain, often praising the scenic vistas, challenging climbs, and the peaceful atmosphere of the natural park.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling options for less experienced riders?
A: While many routes are difficult, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991328807/zufre-embalse-de-zufre-circular-in-the-sierra-de-aracena-y-picos-de-aroche-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zufre, Embalse de Zufre – circular in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.1-mile route that offers beautiful scenery without the most extreme climbs, making it suitable for those looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Zufre?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Zufre serves as a great starting point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994025282/zufre-santa-olalla-and-al" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zufre, Santa Olalla and Al&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, covers 68.1 miles (109.5 km), providing a substantial challenge and an extended exploration of the natural park.
tips: From this viewpoint at the bottom of the park you can see the Zufre reservoir.
Views of the capricious shapes of the Zufre Reservoir - a little to the east of this point there is a path to the dam.
Next to the mountain pass called Puerto de las Eras, is this 18th century hermitage and a recreational area with benches and tables where you can rest for a while.
